metadata.notes: agent_supports: yes (gemini-cli operates on a tier/quota billing model ‚Äî OAuth login-with-google free tier, gemini-api-key/vertex paid; it tracks paid_tier/free_tier + 429/RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED quota) ‚Äî daemon_capability: incapable (NONE of that reaches the tailed .jsonl; the geminicli adapter wires neither of irrlicht's two rate-limit transports) ‚Äî driver_capability: ready (moot) -> route frozen

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: incapable; driver_capability: ready (moot)
-> route: frozen

## The scenario's assertion

The user-observable signal is whether the daemon surfaces a per-session subscription / billing-model reading: a non-nil `RateLimitSnapshot` (>=1 `Window`, or a non-empty `PlanType` / non-nil `Credits` for the codex variant) for a subscription session, vs. a nil snapshot for an API-key session. Two sibling adapters record this row, each via a DIFFERENT daemon surface:

- **claudecode = full** ‚Äî the subscription `rate_limits` block arrives OUT-OF-BAND via the statusline HTTP hook (`POST /api/v1/hooks/claudecode/statusline`); `statuslineToSnapshot()` turns it into a `RateLimitSnapshot`, nil when the block is absent (API-key path). (claudecode/statusline.go.)
- **codex = full** ‚Äî the `rate_limits` map (+ `plan_type` / `credits`) arrives IN-BAND inside the JSONL `token_count` event; `extractCodexRateLimits()` reads it straight off the tailed transcript (codex/parser.go:96,375-423). No hook needed.

So irrlicht has TWO working transports for this signal ‚Äî an HTTP statusline receiver and an in-band JSONL `rate_limits` field ‚Äî but BOTH are adapter-specific, and gemini-cli wires NEITHER.

## Why agent=yes

gemini-cli v0.46.0 unambiguously operates on a tier/quota billing model and is internally aware of subscription state. A keyword scan of the installed bundle finds dense subscription/quota machinery: `subscription` (317), `paidTier` (124), `freeTier` (36), `rate_limit` (186), `quotaRemaining` (30), `quotaExceeded` (12), `429` (817), plus the four auth modes it supports ‚Äî `login-with-google` / `oauth-personal` (the Code Assist free tier), `gemini-api-key`, `vertex-ai`, `cloud-shell`. The CLI tracks paid-vs-free tier and handles 429 / RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED quota exhaustion with retry/backoff. By the higher-bar rule (the surface need not be a top-level flag ‚Äî internal tier/quota state counts), agent=yes is honest: gemini genuinely runs on a billed tier and knows its quota posture.

## Why daemon=incapable (the load-bearing verdict -> frozen)

The block is entirely on the daemon pillar, and it is architectural, not a fixable bug. Three independent, cited reasons:

1. **The signal never reaches the Source the daemon tails.** gemini-cli's Source is `FilesUnderRoot{Dir: ~/.gemini/tmp, JSONLineParser}` (geminicli/agent.go:46-51) ‚Äî the daemon tails ONLY the per-session `chats/*.jsonl`. Each transcript line is a session header `{sessionId, projectHash, startTime, kind}`, a `{"$set":{...}}` envelope (bootstrap messages / lastUpdated heartbeat), or a bare message `{id, type, content, tokens, toolCalls, model}` (parser.go:11-50). There is NO rate-limit, quota, plan_type, tier, credit, or subscription field anywhere in that vocabulary. A grep over ALL real on-disk gemini transcripts under ~/.gemini/tmp for `rate_limit|quota|plan_type|subscription|tier` returns ZERO hits ‚Äî there is no event in `events.jsonl` that could prove a per-session subscription-vs-API-key reading, because no such field is in the tailed transport.

2. **The rate-limit/quota machinery lives in the HTTP/error path, never the chat-record writer.** The bundle's subscription/quota handling is concentrated in the request retry/error path (RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED, retryDelay/retryDelayMultiplier, 429 backoff) and UI/telemetry ‚Äî not in the transcript-record serializer. The chat-record writer validates only `id` / `sessionId` / `projectHash` string properties; it persists message text, tokens, toolCalls, and model, and nothing rate-limit-shaped. The signal is computed at HTTP-response time and discarded from the persisted log.

3. **The geminicli adapter wires NO quota/statusline/capacity integration.** A repo-wide grep of `core/adapters/inbound/agents/geminicli/` for `rate.?limit|quota|subscription|plan.?type|statusline|RateLimit` matches ZERO lines ‚Äî no handlers, no RateLimit-bearing event. The parser emits only `user_message / assistant_message / turn_done / function_call_output`, never a `RateLimit`-bearing event. The adapter registers a SINGLE permission, `transcripts` (agent.go:52-67) ‚Äî no quota/statusline permission exists for it. There is no statusline HTTP receiver registered for gemini (the statusline hook path is claudecode-only, hardwired to the `claudecode/statusline` route + the claudecode transcript_path shape).

Neither claudecode's nor codex's transport applies: gemini emits no in-band `rate_limits` JSONL field (unlike codex), and the gemini CLI is not configured/able to POST a statusline payload to the daemon (unlike claudecode's `statusLine.command` hook). The 3-state model + the tailed JSONL simply cannot represent a subscription reading for gemini. That is `daemon=incapable` ‚Äî no trace exists in the Source ‚Äî not `daemon=bug` (no mis-handled trace) and not a driver gap (no recipe step is missing; the signal itself is absent).

## driver ‚Äî moot under daemon=incapable

The gemini interactive driver is real and complete (driver-interactive.sh step types: send / wait_turn / sleep / slash / keys / session / reset_session / restart / resume / interrupt / sigkill / start_session / exit_clean / session) so the driver pillar is `ready`, but it is moot: there is no observation to elicit. No recipe would make the absent signal appear.

## Disposition

Frozen. gemini-cli genuinely runs on a tier/quota billing model (agent=yes), but no subscription/quota/rate-limit signal reaches the `chats/*.jsonl` the daemon tails, and the geminicli adapter wires neither of irrlicht's two existing transports (statusline hook / in-band rate_limits), so irrlicht fundamentally cannot observe a per-session subscription reading for gemini today. Re-assessable only if (a) a future gemini release writes a rate-limit/plan/tier/quota field into the tailed `.jsonl`, OR (b) a gemini statusline-style POST hook is added, AND the geminicli adapter grows matching ingestion (a snapshot mapping into RateLimitSnapshot).
caveats: agent=yes for the RIGHT reason: gemini-cli operates on a tier/quota billing model (paid_tier/free_tier, OAuth Code-Assist free tier vs gemini-api-key/vertex, 429/RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED quota handling) and is internally aware of subscription state ‚Äî but NONE of that reaches the daemon. The block is purely daemon=incapable, not agent.

irrlicht HAS two working transports for this signal (claudecode's out-of-band statusline hook; codex's in-band JSONL rate_limits field) ‚Äî gemini wires NEITHER. This is architecture (incapable), not a mis-handled trace (bug) and not a missing driver step (gap).

The rate-limit/quota machinery is dense in the gemini bundle but lives entirely in the HTTP request retry/error path and UI/telemetry ‚Äî it is computed at response time and never persisted to the chat-record JSONL the daemon tails (grep of all real ~/.gemini/tmp transcripts for rate_limit|quota|plan_type|subscription|tier = zero hits).
Structurally identical to the kiro-cli subscription-detection cell (agent=yes / daemon=incapable / frozen): the behavior exists but is invisible to the tailed transport.

Provisional only in the trivial sense ‚Äî daemon=incapable is grounded in the transcript schema + adapter code + empty transcript grep, not a plausible parser read; a recording cannot change it because there is no signal to record (frozen, no recording produced).

Re-assess only if a future gemini release surfaces a rate-limit/plan/tier/quota field in the tailed .jsonl (or a gemini statusline POST hook lands) AND the geminicli adapter adds matching RateLimitSnapshot ingestion.

sources.note: Source = FilesUnderRoot{Dir: ~/.gemini/tmp, JSONLineParser} ‚Äî the daemon tails ONLY the chats/*.jsonl (no rate-limit/quota trace). The adapter registers a SINGLE permission, 'transcripts'; there is no quota/statusline permission or handler for gemini.

Transcript vocabulary = session header {sessionId,projectHash,startTime,kind} + $set envelopes + bare messages {id,type,content,tokens,toolCalls,model}. Parser emits only user_message/assistant_message/turn_done/function_call_output; NO model/rate-limit/quota/plan/credit field exists and no RateLimit-bearing event is ever produced.
ZERO matches ‚Äî the geminicli adapter wires no statusline receiver, no rate-limit ingestion, no quota/capacity integration.

ZERO matches over ALL real on-disk gemini transcripts ‚Äî no subscription/rate-limit/quota/plan/tier field is ever persisted to the tailed chat log; the per-session subscription reading is absent from the Source the daemon reads.

Dense hits in the installed bundle (subscription 317, paidTier 124, rate_limit 186, freeTier 36, quotaRemaining 30, quotaExceeded 12, 429 817) plus auth modes login-with-google/oauth-personal/gemini-api-key/vertex-ai ‚Äî gemini-cli DOES handle tier/quota internally (agent=yes), but the context is the HTTP retry/error path (RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED, retryDelay/retryDelayMultiplier), not the chat-record writer.

Reference transport #1 (claudecode=full): the subscription rate_limits block arrives OUT-OF-BAND via POST /api/v1/hooks/claudecode/statusline; statuslineToSnapshot() builds a RateLimitSnapshot (nil when the block is absent). This route is claudecode-specific (hardwired route + claudecode transcript_path shape); gemini has no equivalent POST hook.

Reference transport #2 (codex=full): the rate_limits map (+ plan_type / credits) arrives IN-BAND inside the JSONL token_count event; extractCodexRateLimits() reads it off the tailed transcript. Gemini's JSONL has no such field, so this in-band path also does not apply.

Structurally identical sibling ‚Äî kiro-cli is also agent=yes (auth IS a subscription) / daemon=incapable (nothing subscription/credit reaches the tailed .jsonl; adapter wires neither transport) / frozen. Same verdict shape and the same load-bearing daemon-pillar reasoning.
